exemplar.title: How does the surface temperature of a heated surface influence the duration of a water dropletâs lifetime before it vaporises completely?

subject.title: Physics
subject.description:
**IB Physics** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **space, time and motion; the particulate nature of matter; wave behaviour; fields; and nuclear and quantum physics**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, data analysis, and scientific investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice questions and data-based questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 1 hour 30 minutes | 30% |
| **Paper 2** | Short-answer and extended-response questions | 1 hour 30 minutes | 2 hours 15 minutes | 50% |
| **Internal Assessment** | Individual scientific investigation | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Data are presented in clear, wellâlabelled tables and graphs with units and uncertainties
Processing (averages, standard deviations, piecewise fitting) is appropriate and accurate for HL level
Communication of data recording and processing is generally clear
weaknesses: Propagation of the Â±2 Â°C temperature uncertainty into derived parameters (e.g. gradient uncertainty) is omitted
Uncertainty on exponential fit parameters is not calculated
Minor formatting slips (small axis labels, typo in equation) detract from overall precision
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While two strands sit in the 5â6 band, consideration of uncertainties shows significant omissions (band 3â4). Overall performance aligns with the upper 3â4 band, meriting a 4 rather than a 5 or 6.

strengths: Research question clearly stated in title and situated within a specific scientific context (Leidenfrost effect and boiling curve)
Detailed, largely unambiguous methodology with numerical values and uncertainties that allow reproducibility
Controls are not only listed but their relevance to heat transfer is explained, with justifications for trial numbers and intervals
weaknesses: Minor ambiguities remain (heated area diameter unspecified) hindering exact replication
Risk assessment identifies hazards but lacks detailed mitigation measures (e.g., protective equipment)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands fall comfortably in the 5â6 band: context is specific and appropriate, methodology reproducible with few ambiguities, and methodological considerations are explained. Minor omissions prevent a perfect score, so a 5 is warranted.

strengths: Conclusion fully consistent with analysis, clearly describing lifetime variations and identifying the Leidenfrost point
Relevant comparison is made to literature evaporationâtime curves, situating findings in accepted scientific context
weaknesses: Comparison would be strengthened by a quantitative check of the Leidenfrost point against literature values
Minor stylistic phrasing issues prevent maximal polish
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Both strands meet the 5â6 descriptors: conclusions are justified and consistent, and context comparisons are relevant and justified. Minor depth and stylistic gaps keep the score at 5.

strengths: Specific methodological weaknesses (droplet fragmentation, emissivity mismatch) are identified and linked to precision/accuracy impacts
Suggested improvements (confinement film, plate emissivity calibration) are realistic and directly address identified errors
weaknesses: Improvements are described but not fully explained or expanded (e.g. direct measurement of plate emissivity)
Discussion of additional mitigation strategies (e.g. protective gear in risk assessment) is limited
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student describes realistic and relevant improvements and identifies specific weaknesses (band 3â4). The explanations, while solid, lack the full depth for the 5â6 band, so a score of 4 is appropriate.

strandSelections.reasoning: The research question is explicitly stated in the title and throughout the text.  The introduction gives a detailed explanation of the Leidenfrost effect, relates it to everyday cooking practice and to the boiling-curve theory, thereby situating the question in a **specific and appropriate scientific context**.  Because the reader understands exactly why the variable (surface temperature) matters and how the droplet lifetime links to different boiling regimes, the context is more than merely broad.  Minor repetition and some extraneous background prevent the highest excellence, but the standard of description comfortably meets the 5-6 band.

Instrument uncertainties are quoted, percentage uncertainties calculated for every mean, and error bars plus max/min-slope method applied to the linear fit.  However, propagation of the Â±2 Â°C temperature error into the gradient is not attempted, and uncertainty on exponential parameters is omitted.  These omissions mean consideration is appropriate but **with some significant gaps**, matching the 3-4 band; the thoroughness shown elsewhere supports the upper value of 4.

The student not only lists but **explains** why each control (droplet volume, water temperature, aluminium plate, release height) is relevant to heat transfer.  Justification for the temperature intervals and number of trials shows awareness of sufficiency.  Uncertainties for both independent and dependent variables are considered during design.  While emissivity was overlooked initially, it is acknowledged later; this small gap keeps the score to 5 rather than 6.

Specific weaknesses are identifiedâdroplet fragmentation causing random error and emissivity mismatch causing systematic errorâand their **relative impacts on precision and accuracy are explained**.  Discussion links each weakness to data variability or temperature mis-reading, fulfilling the 5-6 band.  Slightly limited quantitative estimation keeps the score to 5.

Apparatus, materials, variable ranges and trial numbers are itemised.  The set-up of the IR thermometer (10:1 spot ratio, 200 mm distance) and the droplet release height are recorded with numerical values and uncertainties.  A numbered procedure allows another student to reproduce the investigation; drying the plate, trial repetition and temperature stabilisation are noted.  **Few ambiguities remain** (e.g. exact plate diameter, stopwatch operator position), so description is almost fully replicable, meriting the top band though not flawless enough for a 6.

Averages, standard deviations, percentage uncertainties, piece-wise curve fitting and determination of slope with uncertainty are executed without numerical error.  The processing addresses the research question directly and the mathematics is appropriate for HL level.  Although the physical meaning of the exponential fits is limited, the calculations themselves are accurate, so the strand satisfies the 5-6 descriptor (score 5).

Improvements are not only realistic but **explained in terms of how they would mitigate the identified errors**: using a confinement film to keep droplet area constant and calibrating/covering the plate with a material of known emissivity.  The student clarifies expected benefits (reduced variability, correct temperature readings).  The depth is strong though limited to two main points, warranting a 5 in the top band.

Raw and processed data are organised in a labelled table with units and uncertainties, graphs carry titled axes and error bars, and functions are written beside plots.  Presentation is **both clear and precise**, although some axis labels on sub-graphs are small and one typo (âv =â) appears.  Overall quality sits solidly in the highest band; minor formatting slips justify 5 rather than 6.

The student compares their graph with a literature evaporation-time curve, linking each boiling regime and key points (CHF, Leidenfrost) explicitly.  The parallels are analysed, demonstrating **relevant and justified comparison to accepted scientific context**.  Depth is good though not exhaustive (no quantitative check of LFP value), so 5 is appropriate.

The conclusion clearly states how droplet lifetime changes in each temperature region and identifies 150 Â°C as the Leidenfrost point.  It is **fully consistent with the processed data** (piece-wise trends and gradient) and references the quantified slope.  Some phrasing about the interceptâs irrelevance is sound.  Minor stylistic issues prevent a 6 but the logic is solid.

annotations.comment: The research question in the title is clearly stated and situated within a specific context (Leidenfrost effect and boiling curve), effectively guiding the reader into the investigation.
Figure 1 caption is minimal; consider expanding to explain what key features illustrate the Leidenfrost effect and why this image supports the context.
The introduction effectively links everyday cooking practice to film boiling theory, giving a precise context for the research question.
References to figures 1â3 clarify conceptual stages but the transition to the boiling curve (figure 3) would benefit from a clearer description of the axes and curve regions.
Equation 1 defines excess temperature but variables ÎTâexcessâ, T and Tâsaturationâ should be explicitly defined in text to ensure reproducibility.

Fourierâs law (Equation 2) should use LaTeX formatting for clarity: e.g. $$
 oldsymbol{q} = -k 
abla T
$$ and define all symbols.
The methodological setup describes thermometer distance and spot size well, but the diameter of the heated area (cookie-cutter) is not specified, hindering exact replication.
Justification for selecting 5 Â°C increments around 150 Â°C shows strong consideration of where significant changes occur in the Leidenfrost regime.
Controlled variables are well explained, particularly the pipette volume calculation, promoting reproducibility through clear numerical reasoning.
Procedure steps are logically ordered, but the list references âsteps 6â10â which misaligns with actual numberingârevise to maintain clarity.
The risk assessment identifies a major hazard but lacks discussion of mitigation measures (e.g., gloves, eye protection).
Uncertainty section calculates percentages well but should propagate the Â±2 Â°C temperature uncertainty into derived parameters (slopes, fit constants).
Table 1 is well formatted with clear headings, units, and uncertainties, supporting precise data recording and processing.
Qualitative observations link the visual droplet behavior at 150 Â°C to the quantitative peak in lifetime, demonstrating strong integration of data types.
Graph 1 would benefit from axis labels at larger font size and direct annotation of piecewise function domains.
The exponential fit for xâ¤130 predicts an asymptote at x=80Â°C which is physically inconsistent; discuss the valid domain for this function.
Graph 2 and Graph 3 are clear, but RÂ² values are not indicated; including them would strengthen support for each fit.
Derivation of slope uncertainty using max/min lines is executed correctly and enhances precision analysis.
Interpretation of linear slope ignores propagation of temperature uncertainty into slope error; consider including this to refine the Â±1.3 value.
Conclusion clearly states how lifetime varies in each regime and identifies 150 Â°C as Leidenfrost point, fully consistent with analysis.
Comparison with Purdue literature curve is insightful but would be strengthened by quantitative comparison of Leidenfrost point values.
Evaluation identifies specific random (droplet fragmentation) and systematic (emissivity) errors and explains their relative impacts on precision and accuracy.
Suggested improvement to confine droplet area is realistic and clearly explained with expected benefit for reducing random error.
Discussion of emissivity introduces StefanâBoltzmann law well, but lacks recommendation to directly measure plate emissivity for calibration.
Work cited list shows a variety of sources but formatting is inconsistent; apply a uniform citation style (e.g. MLA or APA) throughout.

rubricData.rubricSpecificInfo: âThe Physics Internal Assessment (IA) is a critical component of the IB Diploma Programme, providing students with the opportunity to design and conduct independent investigations into physics phenomena. This assessment emphasizes the development of practical skills, analytical thinking, and a deep understanding of scientific methodologies. By engaging in the IA, students not only apply theoretical knowledge to real-world scenarios but also cultivate competencies in research design, data analysis, and critical evaluation, all of which are essential for academic and professional success in scientific fields. â
rubricData.name: Physics IA
